seasonArc:
Jay Vine's 2026 began with the Tour Down Under overall — a textbook UAE GC win on the team's reliable summer-Aussie template, with the closing kicker the now-iconic kangaroo collision on Stage 5 that gave him a broken wrist on the way to the jersey. The wrist was the story for two months: he sat out the early-spring block, returned to racing at Volta a Catalunya in March still managing pain, and then crashed again on Stage 3 of that race and abandoned — eight weeks of recovery cut short by a second incident inside the wrist's healing window.

The Giro d'Italia in May was meant to be the rebuild's payoff: a return to GC on a stage race Vine had targeted for years. Instead, Stage 2 in Bulgaria produced UAE's worst day of 2026 — a mass crash inside 21 km to go that took out four of the team's riders. Vine fractured an elbow and suffered a concussion, was immediately withdrawn, and joined Soler (fractured pelvis), Adam Yates (concussion), and Baroncini on UAE's mid-May injury list. The TDU win is the year's bright spot; everything since has been recovery interrupted by re-injury.

healthStatus.currentSummary: Out since 9 May 2026 (Giro d'Italia Stage 2 mass crash). Fractured elbow plus concussion; immediately withdrawn from the Giro. Compounds a difficult spring that already included a broken wrist from the Tour Down Under (Jan) and a separate crash and abandonment at Volta a Catalunya (Mar). No public return date.

timeline.label: Kangaroo collision, broken wrist
timeline.description: On Stage 5 of the Tour Down Under, Vine had a close encounter with a kangaroo that crossed the road and suffered a broken wrist. Still went on to win the overall GC. Sat out the early-spring block recovering.

timeline.label: Return at Volta a Catalunya
timeline.description: Returned to racing at Volta a Catalunya, eight weeks after the TDU crash. Told Cyclingnews he was 'still dealing with pain' in the wrist but had 'made a lot of progression' since the immediate aftermath.

timeline.label: Crash and abandon at Volta a Catalunya
timeline.description: Crashed on Stage 3 of Volta a Catalunya around 70 km from the finish. Abandoned. Reports describe the return-from-injury as 'cut short'; no new fracture publicly specified, but he didn't race for a stretch afterward.

timeline.label: Giro Stage 2 mass crash — broken elbow + concussion
timeline.description: On Stage 2 of the Giro d'Italia in Bulgaria, UAE Team Emirates-XRG were 'the worst affected' by a mass crash inside 21 km to go. Vine fractured an elbow and suffered a concussion; immediately withdrawn from the Giro. Same incident: Soler (fractured pelvis), Adam Yates (concussion), and others.

timeline.label: Still out
timeline.description: CyclingNews reports UAE Team Emirates-XRG has at least five riders 'currently unable to race' including Vine with the broken elbow. No revised return timeline issued.

healthStatus.expectedReturn: TBD — no public return date as of mid-May 2026
